questions.text: Which 3 elements of lower extremity positioning help minimize the risk of nerve injury during high lithotomy positioning in gynecologic laparoscopy? Select 3 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Hip abduction ≤90°
B) Hip flexion of 80–100°
C) Lower leg aligned with contralateral shoulder
D) Foot positioned with heel fully supported in boot stirrup
E) External rotation of hips to widen exposure

questions.explanation: Safe high lithotomy limits hip abduction (≤90°), keeps hip flexion ~80–100°, and supports the heel in the stirrup to avoid peroneal compression. Excess abduction/external rotation increase neuropathy risk.

questions.text: During gynecologic laparoscopy in the high lithotomy position, which 2 nerves are most commonly injured due to improper lower extremity positioning? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Obturator nerve
B) Common peroneal nerve
C) Sciatic nerve
D) Femoral nerve
E) Lateral femoral cutaneous nerve

questions.explanation: Common peroneal nerve is vulnerable to compression at the fibular head; femoral nerve can be stretched/compressed under the inguinal ligament with excessive flexion/abduction/external rotation.

questions.text: Which of the following statements about upper extremity positioning are correct? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Arms should be abducted to at least 90 degrees
B) Foam padding should be placed along the arms
C) Arms should be tucked with thumbs pointing down
D) Shoulder braces should be avoided in Trendelenburg
E) Sheets should secure the arms under the torso

questions.explanation: Use padding to reduce compression/shear; avoid shoulder braces in Trendelenburg due to brachial plexus risk. Arms should not be abducted >90° and thumbs should point up with neutral wrist.

questions.text: Which 3 elements help prevent patient slippage during Trendelenburg positioning? Select 3 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Shoulder braces placed tightly above clavicles
B) Foam antiskid materials beneath the body
C) Beanbag positioning devices
D) Compressive chest straps
E) Minimizing steep Trendelenburg angle when possible

questions.explanation: Use antiskid foam and beanbags; reduce the angle when feasible. Shoulder braces/chest straps risk compression injuries and should be avoided or used cautiously.

questions.text: Which of the following are recommended ergonomic adjustments for surgeons during laparoscopic gynecologic surgery? Select all that apply.

answerOptions.text: A) Monitor positioned directly in front of surgeon
B) Elbows flexed at ~90 degrees
C) Instruments positioned at shoulder height
D) Spine rotated slightly for pelvic access
E) Use of footrest or step stool if table height is too high

questions.explanation: Keep the monitor directly ahead at/just below eye level; elbows ~90°; instruments at elbow height; neutral spine; use a step if needed to keep shoulders relaxed.

questions.text: Which 2 ergonomic errors most increase the risk of neck and shoulder injury for the surgeon during laparoscopy? Select 2 best answers.

answerOptions.text: A) Monitors placed below eye level
B) Reaching with arms elevated
C) Elbows flexed at 90 degrees
D) Neck in constant extension
E) Instruments placed at elbow height

questions.explanation: Arm elevation above shoulder level and sustained neck extension drive musculoskeletal strain. Keep instruments at elbow height and favor a slight downward gaze.

questions.text: Which nerve is most commonly injured during gynecologic surgery due to excessive hip flexion and abduction?

answerOptions.text: A) Sciatic nerve
B) Obturator nerve
C) Common peroneal nerve
D) Femoral nerve
E) Lateral femoral cutaneous nerve

questions.explanation: Femoral neuropathy is the most frequent iatrogenic lower-extremity nerve injury in gynecologic surgery, often from excessive hip flexion/abduction/external rotation.

questions.text: Which of the following positioning strategies best reduces the risk of brachial plexus injury during laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Arms fully abducted and secured with chest straps
B) Use of candy cane stirrups and arm boards
C) Tucking the arms at the sides with palms down or thumbs up and foam padding
D) Shoulder braces with Trendelenburg positioning
E) Allowing arms to rest at an obtuse angle from the torso

questions.explanation: Tucking with padding and neutral hand position minimizes brachial plexus/ulnar nerve stretch or compression; shoulder braces and wide abduction increase risk.

questions.text: Which positioning element is most likely to contribute to common peroneal nerve injury?

answerOptions.text: A) Excessive hip flexion
B) Direct pressure on the lateral knee
C) Excessive external rotation of the hip
D) Shoulder braces
E) Compression at the medial thigh

questions.explanation: The common peroneal nerve is superficial at the fibular head and vulnerable to compression from lateral knee pressure or poorly placed stirrups.

questions.text: What is the optimal position for the surgeon's elbows during laparoscopic gynecologic surgery to reduce strain?

answerOptions.text: A) Fully extended
B) At 45-degree angle
C) Slightly elevated from the torso
D) Resting at 90-degree flexion
E) Slightly posterior to the thorax

questions.explanation: Elbows near 90° flexion with instruments at elbow height reduces shoulder load and improves precision.

questions.text: Which of the following best minimizes cervical spine strain during laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: A) Monitor positioned above the surgeon’s eye level
B) Surgeon positioned one step above assistant
C) Monitor aligned with surgeon’s shoulder and promoting a 15-degree downward gaze
D) Instruments held above chest level
E) Use of angled headlamp to view field

questions.explanation: A monitor straight ahead and slightly below eye level encourages neutral neck with gentle downward gaze, minimizing extension.

questions.text: Which ergonomic deviation is most associated with increased shoulder and neck strain in the operating room?

answerOptions.text: A) Neck in mild flexion with neutral spine
B) Feet flat and hips even
C) Elevation of upper extremities away from torso
D) Slight trunk lean forward
E) Forearms resting on surgical tray

questions.explanation: Sustained arm elevation/abduction increases shoulder and neck load; keep arms close to torso with instruments at elbow height.
